Localization.Description: Password spraying in the GitLab version control system. Password spraying is a type of brute-force attack in which an attacker tries the most popular passwords sequentially for each user, which helps avoid blocking the accounts of the attacked users.

EventDescriptions.EventDescription: Successful password spraying in application {event_src.title} on host {event_src.host}. The passwords of users {datafield2} are bruteforced. Number of failed authorization attempts: {datafield1}.

Successful password spraying in application {event_src.title} on host {event_src.host}. The password of user {subject.account.name} is bruteforced. Number of failed authorization attempts: {datafield1}.
Password spraying attempts in application {event_src.title} on host {event_src.host}. Number of authorization attempts: {datafield1}.
